content:
[Manual] MAF Sensor Diagnostic Procedure
Category: diagnostic
Source: OEM Manual
DTC: P0101
Content: When DTC P0101 is detected, inspect the mass air flow sensor for dust contamination, oil residue, or signal instability. Check sensor connector pin contact, verify harness locking condition, and inspect the intake duct for unmetered air leakage. If fuel trim is significantly positive and airflow value is unstable, clean the sensor and re-check signal consistency before replacement.

[Manual] Intake Air Leak Inspection Guide
Category: maintenance
Source: Internal QA Guide
DTC: P0101
Content: For repeated airflow range/performance faults, inspect the intake hose, clamp looseness, and downstream air leak path. Confirm whether ECU-calculated airflow differs from expected engine load. If connector contact is normal and contamination is low, prioritize intake leak inspection.

[Manual] ECU Processor Fault Initial Check
Category: diagnostic
Source: OEM Manual
DTC: P0606
Content: When DTC P0606 occurs, verify ECU power stability, connector seating, and processor fault recurrence. Check whether thermal load, supply voltage instability, or abnormal reset behavior is present. If the fault persists under repeated high temperature operation, consider ECU replacement review.

[Manual] ECU Thermal Management Inspection Procedure
Category: safety
Source: Internal QA Guide
DTC: P0606
Content: For rising ECU temperature conditions, inspect cooling airflow around the ECU housing, connector heat damage, and local thermal concentration. If temperature continues to rise beyond the internal acceptance range, suspend continuous validation and escalate for hardware review.

[Manual] Cylinder Misfire Diagnostic Manual
Category: diagnostic
Source: OEM Manual
DTC: P0302
Content: When DTC P0302 is detected, inspect cylinder 2 ignition coil, spark plug condition, and injector firing quality. Review RPM fluctuation and abnormal combustion indicators. Perform coil swap test and verify whether the misfire follows the component.

[Manual] Ignition Coil and Spark Plug Inspection Guide
Category: maintenance
Source: Internal QA Guide
DTC: P0302
Content: Repeated cylinder 2 misfire may result from ignition coil degradation, spark plug wear, or injector imbalance. Inspect plug electrode wear, secondary ignition leakage, and unstable combustion symptoms before replacing ECU-related control parts.

metadata.title: Ignition Coil and Spark Plug Inspection Guide

content:
[Case] #1 DTC=P0101
Symptom: Engine response became weak during validation and airflow reading fluctuated abnormally.
RootCause: MAF sensor contamination caused unstable airflow signal input to ECU.
ActionSteps: 1) Disconnect connector and inspect terminals. 2) Clean MAF sensing element. 3) Recheck airflow signal and fuel trim.
ResultSummary: After sensor cleaning, airflow stabilized and fuel trim moved toward normal range.

[Case] #2 DTC=P0101
Symptom: Repeated airflow performance fault occurred after maintenance work.
RootCause: Intake hose leak introduced unmetered air, causing ECU airflow estimation mismatch.
ActionSteps: 1) Inspect intake duct and clamp. 2) Remove leak source. 3) Re-run OBD check and confirm DTC clear.
ResultSummary: Air leak was removed and repeated P0101 event did not recur.

[Case] #3 DTC=P0606
Symptom: ECU fault triggered repeatedly during thermal validation cycle.
RootCause: Cooling airflow around ECU housing was insufficient, leading to thermal stress on processor area.
ActionSteps: 1) Check ECU mounting and airflow path. 2) Inspect connector heat marks. 3) Pause test and cool down ECU.
ResultSummary: Temperature rise slowed after airflow correction and no immediate recurrence was observed.

[Case] #4 DTC=P0606
Symptom: Processor fault persisted under high temperature despite normal external connector fit.
RootCause: Internal ECU processor instability under sustained thermal load.
ActionSteps: 1) Verify supply voltage. 2) Reproduce under controlled heat condition. 3) Review ECU replacement.
ResultSummary: Fault was isolated to ECU internal condition and replacement recommendation was issued.

[Case] #5 DTC=P0302
Symptom: Engine vibration increased and combustion became unstable during line inspection.
RootCause: Cylinder 2 ignition coil degradation caused intermittent misfire.
ActionSteps: 1) Swap ignition coil. 2) Recheck cylinder-specific misfire. 3) Replace faulty coil.
ResultSummary: Misfire moved with the coil during swap test, confirming component issue.

[Case] #6 DTC=P0302
Symptom: OBD detected cylinder 2 misfire with unstable idle and rough acceleration.
RootCause: Spark plug wear and partial combustion instability were identified.
ActionSteps: 1) Inspect spark plug electrode. 2) Replace worn plug. 3) Confirm injector balance if symptom remains.
ResultSummary: Replacing the spark plug reduced misfire count and stabilized idle RPM.
